models.Component: An application component with its Azure infrastructure configuration details.
models.ComponentConfiguration: The Azure service configuration for a component including service type, SKU, settings, and other infrastructure details.
models.DeploymentStrategy: The unified deployment approach for all components including CI/CD setup, environments, and deployment process.
models.ArchitectureDiagram: Visual representation of the architecture showing resource groups, resources, and connections between components.

relationships: ArchitectureDiagram visualizes all Components and their relationships

components.name: Web Frontend
components.description: React-based single-page application that provides the customer-facing interface

components.name: API Backend
components.description: RESTful API service that handles business logic and integrates with on-premises systems

components.name: Background Worker
components.description: Processes asynchronous tasks like email notifications and report generation

components.name: Customer Database
components.description: Stores customer profiles, preferences, and authentication data

components.name: Document Storage
components.description: Stores customer documents for download and reference

components.name: Session Cache
components.description: Distributed cache for user sessions and API response caching

components.name: Application Gateway
components.description: Entry point for incoming HTTPS traffic with SSL termination and WAF

deployment.content:
# Deployment Strategy

## Source Control
- **Repository**: GitHub (github.com/contoso/customer-portal)
- **Branching**: GitFlow (main, develop, feature branches)
- **Protection**: Branch protection on main and develop

## CI/CD Platform
- **Platform**: GitHub Actions
- **Workflows**: Separate workflows per component
- **Secrets**: Stored in GitHub Secrets, synced from Azure Key Vault

## Environments
- **Development**: Auto-deploy from develop branch
- **Staging**: Manual approval required
- **Production**: Manual approval + change management ticket

## Deployment Process
1. Developer creates PR to develop branch
2. Automated tests and security scans run on PR
3. Merge triggers deployment to Development environment
4. Staging deployment requires approval from tech lead
5. Production deployment requires approval from change board
6. All deployments use infrastructure-as-code (Bicep)
7. Rollback capability via GitHub Actions rerun with previous commit

## Quality Gates
- Unit tests must pass (80% coverage minimum)
- Integration tests must pass
- Security scan (no high/critical vulnerabilities)
- Infrastructure validation (Bicep lint + what-if)
- Performance tests (staging only)
